cards.back: A digraph is a type of graph in which there is a directed edge between every pair of vertices.

cards.front: What is a binary relation in graph theory?
cards.back: A binary relation between two vertices in a graph is a relation that can be expressed by a directed edge between them.

cards.front: What is the difference between digraphs and undirected graphs?
cards.back: Digraphs are directed graphs, meaning that edges have direction, whereas undirected graphs do not.

cards.front: What is the definition of a homogeneous relation in graph theory?
cards.back: A homogeneous relation in graph theory is a binary relation that is symmetric and transitive.

cards.front: What is the definition of a Digraph being connected?
cards.back: In a digraph, a set of vertices is connected if there is a path from every vertex to every other vertex.
